甲.wiki_summary: Pictogram of the protective hard shell, carapace, or scales of an animal, or a suit of armor made of connected plates.
甲.note: Wiktionary reads 甲 as a carapace or suit of armor (Proto-Sino-Tibetan *krap "shell/shield"), not a sprouting seed shell as stated in our data.

虫.wiki_summary: Ideogrammic compound (triplication of 虫 'snake; insect') — three 虫 stacked to form 蟲.
虫.note: The simplified 虫 is itself a pictograph (snake), but its traditional form 蟲 is an ideogrammic compound (3×虫). Our formation describes the component 虫, not the traditional character 蟲.

言.wiki_summary: Ideogram (指事) from 口 plus ▽ (possibly original form of 弋, marking location); later mixed with 舌/辛/䇂
言.note: Both classify as simple indicative. Wiktionary describes the marker as a tongue-position indicator above 口 (mouth), slightly different from our "tongue speaking" reading.

广.wiki_summary: Phono-semantic compound: semantic 广 + phonetic 黃 (OC *ɡʷaːŋ); 廣 reconstructed as OC *kʷaːŋʔ
广.note: Wiktionary classifies 廣 as a phono-semantic compound: 广 (cliff/shelter, semantic) + 黃 (phonetic). Our data treats it as a simple pictograph of a mountain-side shelter.

食.wiki_summary: Ideogrammic compound: 亼 (mouth) + 皀 (bowl of rice on a stand)
食.note: Wiktionary classifies 食 as an ideogrammic compound: 亼 (mouth) + 皀 (food in a vessel). Our data incorrectly assigns 皂 as a phonetic component, making it phono-semantic.

又.wiki_summary: Pictogram of a right hand; original form of 右
又.note: Both classify as pictograph. Wiktionary clarifies the depicted object is a right hand (original form of 右), not "repetition" as our note implies.

足.wiki_summary: Pictogram of a leg, similar to 疋.
足.note: Wiktionary reads 足 as a unified pictograph of a leg and foot, not a compound of separate semantic components (止 + knee).

干.wiki_summary: Originally 榦, phono-semantic compound: phonetic 倝 + semantic 木 (tree); later 木 replaced by phonetic 干.
干.note: Simplified 干 merges three traditional chars: 幹 (trunk/manage, phono-semantic: 倝+木), 乾 (dry, phono-semantic), and 干 (shield/offend, pictograph). Our formation describes the 干 (shield) sense only.

甘.wiki_summary: Indicative (指事): depicts something sweet inside an open mouth.
甘.note: Wiktionary classifies 甘 as a simple indicative (指事): the stroke inside 口 is an abstract position marker for "something sweet in the mouth," not a second semantic component making it a compound ideograph.

十.wiki_summary: No explicit formation type given; glyph origin describes a single vertical stroke in oracle bone script, with theories of pictogram (needle borrowed for numeral) or knotted-cord form — 指事 not mentioned
十.note: Formation origin uncertain. Wiktionary does not confirm the indicative reading; theories include a borrowed pictogram of a needle or a knotted cord used for counting.

皮.wiki_summary: Pictogram: depicts a hand (又) stripping fur from an animal pelt; the pelt moved to top-left and became abstracted
皮.note: Wiktionary classifies 皮 as a pictograph: a unified depiction of a hand stripping an animal pelt, not an ideogrammic compound of independent semantic parts.

亡.wiki_summary: Ideogram (指事): a line on a knife depicting its cutting edge; original form of 芒.
亡.note: Wiktionary classifies 亡 as a simple indicative: a mark on a knife blade indicating the hidden/absent edge — original form of 芒. Our data's compound ideograph analysis (入+乚 = person hiding) is a later folk reading.

旦.wiki_summary: Phono-semantic compound (形聲): semantic 日 (sun/day) + phonetic 丁; Shuowen's sun-over-horizon reading is considered folk etymology.
旦.note: Type mismatch: we classify as simple_indicative based on the traditional sun-over-horizon reading; Wiktionary classifies as phono_semantic_compound with 丁 as phonetic component, treating the indicative reading as folk etymology.

示.note: Wiktionary classifies 示 as a pictograph: a direct depiction of a ritual altar or spirit tablet, not an ideogrammic compound of separate semantic parts.

古.wiki_summary: Indicative (指事): shield + 口 (mouth) indicating 'strong/firm'; original form of 固
古.note: Wiktionary classifies 古 as a simple indicative, with the top element reading as a contracted shield (not 十), meaning "firm/strong" — original form of 固. The "ten mouths = ancient" folk reading in our data is not etymologically supported.

申.wiki_summary: Pictogram (象形): depicts a bolt of lightning; original form of 電 (lightning) and 神 (deity)
申.note: Wiktionary classifies 申 as a pictograph of a lightning bolt, not an indicative of an upright bound body. The lightning interpretation aligns with its original meaning "9th earthly branch" and related character 電.

舌.wiki_summary: Pictogram (象形): a snake's tongue emerging upward from a mouth (口)
舌.note: Wiktionary reads 舌 as a pictograph of a snake's forked tongue above a mouth — a unified image, not an ideogrammic compound of 口 + 干.

之.wiki_summary: Ideogrammic compound: 止 (foot) + 一 (starting place) — originally meaning 'to go'
之.note: Wiktionary identifies the components as 止 (foot) + 一 (ground), meaning "to go/proceed." Our data's sprouting-plant description misidentifies the character; that imagery belongs to 生 or 屮.

黑.wiki_summary: Pictogram (象形): depicts a covered head or face in oracle bone script; dots added in bronze inscriptions
黑.note: Wiktionary classifies 黑 as a pictograph of a face or head covered in soot/markings. Our chimney+fire compound reading is not supported by glyph analysis.

夕.wiki_summary: Pictogram of a moon crescent; compare ancient variant 月 (full moon).
夕.note: Wiktionary classifies 夕 as a pictograph of a crescent moon — same imagery as our note, but formation type is pictograph not simple indicative.

韦.wiki_summary: Ideogrammic compound: 舛 + 囗, depicting footsteps around an enclosure; possibly original form of 圍/衛, borrowed for 'tanned leather'.
韦.note: Wiktionary classifies 韋 as an ideogrammic compound: 舛 (contrary footsteps) + 囗 (enclosure) = feet going opposite ways around a wall. Our data treats 囗 as a phonetic, making it phono-semantic — incorrect.

谷.wiki_summary: Wiktionary page for traditional form 穀 (grain): phono-semantic compound, semantic 禾 (grain) + phonetic 𣪊.
谷.note: Simplified 谷 covers two unrelated traditional characters: 谷 (valley, ideogrammic compound) and 穀 (grain, phono-semantic: 禾+𣪊). These were merged in simplification; our formation addresses only the valley sense.

向.wiki_summary: Wiktionary page for 向: ideogrammic compound 宀 (house) + 口 (mouth/opening), representing echoes in a place; original form of 響, later borrowed for 嚮 (toward). Page for 嚮 shows it is ⿱鄉向, a further derived form.
向.note: Wiktionary classifies 向 as an ideogrammic compound: 宀 (dwelling) + 口 (opening/window) = a north-facing window. Our data calls it a pictograph.

垂.wiki_summary: Pictogram: 𠂹 (flowers hanging from a plant) + 土 (earth), depicting plants drooping downward.
垂.note: Wiktionary classifies 垂 as a pictograph of hanging flowers drooping toward the ground — a unified image. Our data incorrectly assigns a phono-semantic structure (土 semantic + 垂 phonetic, which is circular).

青.wiki_summary: Phono-semantic compound: semantic 生 (growth of plants), phonetic 井; Shuowen's ideogrammic reading (生+丹) noted but not adopted.
青.note: Wiktionary (citing Zhang 2022) classifies 青 as a phono-semantic compound: 生 (semantic, growth/plant) + 井 (phonetic). The Shuowen compound ideograph reading (生+丹) is an older analysis.

走.wiki_summary: Pictogram of a running man; 止 (foot) added later; top component is 夭.
走.note: Wiktionary classifies 走 as a pictograph of a running person — a unified pictorial image, not an ideogrammic compound of person + foot parts.

士.wiki_summary: Pictogram of a war axe; later took meanings of soldier and intellectual; related to 王 and 圭.
士.note: Wiktionary classifies 士 as a pictograph of a war axe. The "一+十 = a man of virtue" compound reading in our data is a folk etymology not supported by glyph analysis.

旨.wiki_summary: Ideogrammic compound (primary): 匕 (spoon) used to eat or drink something delicious. Shuowen gives secondary phono-semantic reading: phonetic 匕 + semantic 甘 ('delicious').
旨.note: Wiktionary's primary classification is ideogrammic compound: 匕 (spoon) + 甘 (sweet) = something delicious eaten with a spoon. The phono-semantic reading (匕 phonetic) is Shuowen's secondary account.

合.wiki_summary: Wiktionary page for traditional form 閤: phono-semantic compound (OC *kuːb): semantic 門 ('door') + phonetic 合. This character means 'side door/small room', not the simplex 合.
合.note: 閤 is a derived character (門+合, phono-semantic) distinct from 合 itself. 合 (ideogrammic compound: 亼+口 = lid closing over mouth) is correct; but the traditional form listed in our data (閤) is a separate word meaning "side door."

束.wiki_summary: Ideogram (指事) – a bag tied at both ends (earlier forms often vertically symmetric); related to 東 and 柬.
束.note: Wiktionary classifies 束 as a simple indicative: a depiction of a bag tied at both ends, with the binding marks as position indicators — not an ideogrammic compound of 囗 (rope) + 木 (wood).

五.wiki_summary: Originally written 𠄡 (㐅 with bars top and bottom); original meaning unclear. Wiktionary's leading theory cites it as a pictogram (palm lines from extending fingers), with no consensus on indicative classification.
五.note: Wiktionary's leading theory classifies 五 as a pictograph (palm lines or a knotted cord representing the number 5), not a simple indicative. Origin uncertain.

莫.wiki_summary: Pictogram of the sun (日) sinking into the bushes (屮), representing dusk; the bottom was later distorted into 大.
莫.note: Wiktionary classifies 莫 as a pictograph: sun sinking into vegetation = dusk, a unified visual image. Our data classifies it as an ideogrammic compound of 日 + 茻 — same imagery but different classification.

千.wiki_summary: Phono-semantic compound (OC *sn̥ʰiːn): semantic 一 ('one, signifying a number') + phonetic 人. Note: Wiktionary page for trad form 韆 (swing/千秋) is a different character.
千.note: Wiktionary classifies 千 as a phono-semantic compound: 一 (semantic) + 人 (phonetic). Our simple indicative reading is not supported. Note: the traditional form 韆 listed in our data means "swing" (千秋), not the numeral — an incorrect trad mapping.

斗.wiki_summary: Wiktionary page for traditional form 鬥: pictogram of two figures face-to-face fighting with bare hands, hair bristled. This is a completely different character from 斗 (ladle/measure).
斗.note: Simplified 斗 merges two unrelated traditional characters: 斗 (ladle/measure, pictograph of a handled vessel) and 鬥 (fight, pictograph of two people wrestling). These are completely different characters with different etymologies.
